steps.title: Find the radius of container B
steps.content:
Container A has radius 16 cm. Container B's radius is 25% longer:
$$r_B = 16 + 0.25(16) = 16 + 4 = 20 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Calculate volume of container B
steps.content:
Substituting $r = 20$ cm and $h = 50$ cm into the cylinder volume formula:
$$V = \pi(20)^2(50) = \pi(400)(50) = 20{,}000\pi \text{ cm}^3$$
That matches Choice B — $20 000 \pi$.
